methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $4M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
SunDrive Solar is a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised A$60M+ including a A$25.3M ARENA grant (November 2025). The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies: Graphyte ($200M Series A), Fourth Power ($150M Series A), and Steady Energy ($110M Series A), supplemented by reference to a broader climate-tech universe of nine private comparables and one public proxy.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to the $4M total-raised proxy and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for seed-to-next-stage transitions, yields $10M. Peer-median analysis across the three comparable companies produces a $150M implied valuation, with a range of $110M to $200M. The substantial spread reflects the nascency of the stage and heterogeneity in funding timing and capital deployment across the cohort.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $8M (low) to $172.5M (high), with a median of $80M. The wide band reflects two competing signals: the stage-progression method suggests earlier-stage positioning near $10M, while peer medians point toward substantially higher valuations typical of later-stage or better-capitalized peers. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing event, capital efficiency relative to peers, and technology-risk maturation in silicon-heterojunction solar cells.
